trade.summary: Bilateral trade has been disrupted by Western sanctions on Russia. Malaysia has not joined sanctions but faces secondary sanction risks. Russia exports fertilizers, steel, and wheat; Malaysia exports electronics, palm oil, and rubber. MH17 tragedy deeply damaged public sentiment.
disputes: MH17 downing by Russian-backed forces (2014) — ongoing legal proceedings at ICJ

diplomatic.summary: Relations severely damaged by the 2014 downing of MH17 over eastern Ukraine, killing 298 people including 44 Malaysian nationals. Malaysia has pursued legal action at the International Court of Justice against Russia. Malaysia abstained on some UN votes condemning Russia but has publicly demanded accountability for MH17. Relations are formally maintained but deeply strained.
recent_incidents: Malaysia demanded Russia's cooperation in MH17 criminal investigation
Malaysia abstained on UN vote condemning Russian invasion of Ukraine

military.summary: Malaysia operates some Russian-origin military equipment including Sukhoi Su-30MKM fighters. Spare parts and maintenance cooperation continues but has become more complicated since 2022. Malaysia has been cautious about expanding defense ties with Russia.

financial.summary: Very limited financial ties. Secondary sanctions risk has largely frozen banking relationships. Russian entities have limited access to Malaysian financial systems.

energy.summary: Both are significant oil and gas producers. Minimal bilateral energy trade. Petronas and Rosneft had exploratory contacts in early 2010s that did not result in major cooperation.

scientific.summary: Very limited scientific cooperation. Academic exchanges have declined sharply since 2014 and further since 2022.
